channel.title: Posts on Diego Strebel
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Diego Strebel

item.title: 'Silencing' the Instinct MI 50
item.description:
The Instinct MI 50

As noted in my previous post about the MI 50, I was plagued by a beeping sound that came directly from the card. Originally, I thought that it must come from the motherboard, since I was not aware of a GPU that had a physical beeper. But during the teardown, I could see that the card indeed has a beeper just like a normal motherboard. It is highlighted in the next picture.

item.title: All the FP64 without CUDA - AMD Radeon Instinct MI50 Review
item.description:
Teardown

Tearing down the card was pretty straightforward. The outer shroud is held on by 6 small screws. When removed, the shroud can be pulled off the card by slightly wiggling it from side to side. Underneath, the card consists of a long black heat sink spanning the whole length of the card. The GPU die is cooled by a copper vapor chamber, easily recognized by its silver fins.

item.title: Cisco Nexus N9K-92160YC-X
item.description:
Introduction

TL;dr

Installation

Setup

Contrary to this post on the ServeTheHome forum, I had no issues installing 10GBase-T transceivers in the SFP28 Slots. Overall, I had great hardware support with the only non-compatible optics being the Intel CWDM 2km which just refused to work.

Caviats

Conclusion

Appendix 0: Fan Swap
